etymology.description: semantic 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") + phonetic 云(云) (yún, "cloud") -> character for "cloud"
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 雨(雨) (yǔ, "rain") to represent the connection to weather, specifically rain, with the phonetic component 云(云) (yún, "cloud") to suggest its pronunciation. This construction clearly connects the idea of rain in the atmosphere with clouds.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form removes the 雨(雨) component which explicitly denotes weather, simplifying to 云, which serves both as a phonetic marker and a representation of the concept.

examples.chinese: 天空中的云很美(天空中的雲很美)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng zhōng de yún hěn měi.
examples.english: The clouds in the sky are beautiful.

examples.chinese: 今天的天气预报说会有很多云(今天的天氣預報說會有很多雲)。
examples.pinyin: Jīntiān de tiānqì yùbào shuō huì yǒu hěn duō yún.
examples.english: Today's weather forecast says there will be a lot of clouds.

examples.chinese: 乌云密布时(烏雲密布時)，看起来好像要下雨(看起來好像要下雨)。
examples.pinyin: Wūyún mìbù shí, kàn qǐlái hǎoxiàng yào xiàyǔ.
examples.english: When the dark clouds gather, it looks like it's going to rain.
